claim: Ultrasound guidance significantly raises the number of traumatic procedures when attempting needle insertion.

result.claim: Ultrasound guidance significantly raises the number of traumatic procedures when attempting needle insertion.
result.reasoning: The claim that ultrasound guidance significantly raises the number of traumatic procedures when attempting needle insertion is directly refuted by a systematic review and meta-analysis (Paper ID: 15482274). This high-quality study found that ultrasound imaging significantly reduced the risk of traumatic procedures (risk ratio 0.27 (0.11 to 0.67), P=0.005). Furthermore, the study concluded that ultrasound imaging can reduce the risk of failed or traumatic lumbar punctures and epidural catheterizations, as well as the number of needle insertions and redirections.

evidence.text: Ultrasound imaging significantly reduced the risk of traumatic procedures (risk ratio 0.27 (0.11 to 0.67), P=0.005).
evidence.source: Ultrasound imaging for lumbar punctures and epidural catheterisations: systematic review and meta-analysis.

evidence.text: Six of the 624 procedures conducted in the ultrasound group failed.
evidence.source: Ultrasound imaging for lumbar punctures and epidural catheterisations: systematic review and meta-analysis.
